hints.Maria: 'Maria' is a proper noun — the subject of the sentence
hints.verba: 'verba' is a noun meaning 'words' in the accusative plural

hints.to observe: 'to observe' is 'observare' — 'conservabat' comes from 'conservare' (to keep safe)
hints.to conserve energy: 'conservare' means 'to keep/preserve' in a broader sense, not the modern technical meaning

hints.Present: Present would be 'conservat' — 'conservabat' with -ba- infix is imperfect
hints.Perfect: Perfect would be 'conservavit' — 'conservabat' with -ba- is imperfect

hints.servo, servare, servavi, servatum: These are the parts of 'servare' (to save/guard) — 'conservare' adds the prefix con-
hints.observo, observare, observavi, observatu: These are the parts of 'observare' (to watch/observe) — different prefix
hints.servio, servire, servivi, servitum: These are the parts of 'servire' (to serve) — a different verb entirely

hints.Nominative: 'verba' could be nominative plural, but here it is the direct object — accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative singular would be 'verbo' — 'verba' is accusative plural neuter
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'verba' (same form), but here it is the direct object of 'conservabat'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'verbi' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: 'omnia' could be nominative neuter plural, but here it agrees with 'verba' as accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'omnibus' — 'omnia' is accusative plural neuter here
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'omnium' — 'omnia' is accusative agreeing with 'verba'

translation: Mary was keeping all these words
